title: Today's AI Brief: The Trump administration might take an equity stake in OpenAI
summary: A brief look at the Trump administration's potential equity stake in OpenAI and Apple's possible plans for AirPods with cameras.
imageAlt: Image from source article for The Trump administration might take an equity stake in OpenAI
imageCaption: The Trump administration might take an equity stake in OpenAI
imageSourceTitle: The Trump administration might take an equity stake in OpenAI

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and welcome to today's AI Brief. I've got a couple of interesting stories to share with you. The Trump administration is considering taking an equity stake in OpenAI, the company behind ChatGPT. According to a report on TechCrunch, President Donald Trump has spoken to AI companies about striking deals 'where the American people can benefit from the success of AI.'

The idea here is that the US government could potentially take a stake in OpenAI, which would give them a financial interest in the company's success. My read is that this could be a way for the government to tap into the potential economic benefits of AI, while also possibly exerting some influence over the company's direction. It's an interesting development, and one that raises questions about the role of government in the AI space.

Also making headlines is a report from Wired that Apple might be planning to put cameras into its next-generation AirPods. Now, I love my AirPods, but adding cameras takes them to a whole new level. If you're already using them to listen to music or podcasts, why not use them to take a selfie or two? The idea might sound far-fetched, but it's hard to deny the convenience of having a tiny camera built right into your earbuds.

My quick read: the artificial intelligence story keeps getting bigger, but also stranger in very human ways. One moment it is strategy and infrastructure; the next, the Vatican is in the room. That is a pretty good sign this technology has left the lab and wandered into the town square.
